feed.title: Amir Hamzah
feed.subtitle: A simple, whitespace theme for academics. Based on [*folio](https://github.com/bogoli/-folio) design.

entry.title: Top 3 in Gamuda AI Academy!
entry.content:
By God’s mercy, we actually won! We finished 3rd out of 8 teams in the Gamuda AI Academy, and I am incredibly proud of what our team achieved. The competition was intense, but every hour we spent preparing made the result feel even more rewarding.

Throughout the cohort, we practiced constantly. Our teammates can probably confirm how many times we stood in front of each other, rehearsing the script until the delivery felt natural and the flow finally clicked.

Another practice session focused on our script, presentation flow, and content before the big day.

We even went the extra mile and met at one of our teammate Raina’s house on the weekend to rehearse our presentation one last time. We wanted to make sure every part of the demo was as polished as possible.

A few days before the big day, we gathered at Raina's house to practice our presentation and demo one last time.

On the big day, everyone suited up and stayed focused. We managed to pitch AIris within the 10-minute time frame given to us, and that alone already felt like a victory.

The team during the big day, pitching AIris to the judges and audience.

One of my personal goals in events like this is to answer at least one question from the judges. I know how difficult it is to think on the spot, so that was a goal I was genuinely nervous about. Did I manage to do it? Yes. Did I cook? Maybe. But more importantly, I overcame the fear of being asked a question and answered it with confidence, and that meant a lot to me.

Me doing my best to answer a question from the judge during the Q&A session.

And the result? We stood on the podium and received our award from John Stefan, the Assistant Manager of Gamuda AI Academy. In that moment, all the hard work, breakdowns, practice sessions, stress, anxiety, fear, excitement, and joy felt worth it.

Award-giving ceremony at the end of the demo day, presented by John Stefan.

Heartfelt message to everyone in the academy

But in the end, I think the most beautiful part of the Gamuda AI Academy was the people I met, especially during Cohort 5. Everyone brought a different story, a different perspective, and a different strength to the academy, and I learned so much from being around such experienced people.

The final group photo for Cohort 5. I will definitely miss every one of them.

I also want to express my deepest gratitude to the trainers, John Stefan, Isaac Ebi, Lee Chee Tat, Muhammad Syazwan, and Mohamed Syafiq, for their teaching, guidance, support, and encouragement throughout the academy. Some of them even got their hands dirty by helping us debug our code and by giving feedback on our presentation. I am truly grateful for their help, and I hope to learn more from them in the future.

The most "masuk-air" trainer, who made learning fun and a lot less stressful.

And to my fellow teammates, I am so grateful to have been part of Team 1. In just three months, we created so many memories together, and I will always carry that with me. I am praying for each and every one of you to achieve your dreams and goals in the future.

Thank you very much Team 1: Gem-in-AI 💖💎

entry.title: We build AIris!
entry.content:
Following my first post about Gamuda AI Academy, I now wanted to share my capstone project that we presented during the demo day. We delivered AIris - A camera-first language learning application, an app designed to help kids struggling with learning and pronouncing words in Bahasa Melayu. It was a team project consisting of six people.

A simple "non-ai" generated logo for AIris.

AIris - A Camera-First Malay Learning Application

The idea is simple: help kids learn to converse in Bahasa Melayu in a fun and engaging way—through the camera lens. Think of it as Pokemon Go, but educational and meaningful.

Core Feature of AIris

The camera feature allows kids to point at an object, take a picture, and learn its name in Bahasa Melayu. For example, if a kid doesn’t know the word for “cat,” they can take a picture, and AIris will recognize the object and teach them how to pronounce it in Bahasa Melayu.

In the background, the image is sent to Gemini API, which recognizes the object and generates a response to teach the user.

The camera feature User Interface (UI) of AIris.

VocabCard - Comprehensive Flashcard System

  • After processing the image, the word is displayed in VocabCard.
  • VocabCard includes:
    • The word itself.
    • A simple description of the object.
    • Pronunciation guide through Text-to-Speech (TTS).
    • Sample sentences demonstrating usage in English and Bahasa Melayu.
VocabCard User Interface (UI) of AIris.

Kids can either take another picture or save the word as a flashcard. Saved words are stored in the wordbank, which they can review later.

WordBank User Interface (UI) of AIris.

Leitner System - The Flashcard Learning Method

To test kids’ understanding of words, AIris offers three types of quizzes:

  1. Naming Test - Name the object in Bahasa Melayu based on the picture.
  2. Listening Test - Listen to the pronunciation and type the correct answer.
  3. Speaking Test - Pronounce the word, and the app checks if it’s correct.

The mixed test combines all three quiz types. Questions are drawn from the word bank based on their exploration of words.

A collection of flashcard quiz User Interface (UI) of AIris.
In-depth overview of all the different types of flashcard quiz User Interface (UI) of AIris.

The Leitner system is a flashcard-based method for memorization and retention. Flashcards are sorted into categories based on familiarity, and reviewed at increasing intervals. Difficult cards are reviewed more frequently, while easier ones are reviewed less often. This helps focus study time on areas needing improvement.

This system was a proof of concept for the capstone project. While we encountered errors implementing the algorithm, we managed to create a simple working version.

Leitner System simple explanation.

Leaderboard - Gamified Learning Experience

To keep kids engaged, we implemented a leaderboard. Kids earn points by taking pictures and answering quizzes. They can see their ranking, encouraging them to keep learning new words in Bahasa Melayu.

Leaderboard User Interface (UI) of AIris.

ChatBot - KSSR-Based Chatbot for Interactive Learning

The chatbot helps kids learn abstract words like “freedom” or “happiness” when they can’t snap images. Kids can ask the chatbot for meanings and pronunciations in Bahasa Melayu. The chatbot generates kid-friendly responses.

We also implemented Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) concepts tailored to the Kurrikulum Standard Sekolah Rendah (KSSR). This allows the chatbot to retrieve information from the KSSR curriculum and generate responses. Kids can ask questions like:

  • What is “Peribahasa”?
  • What is “Rukun Negara”?
  • Teach me about “penjodoh bilangan”.
RAG-based ChatBot User Interface (UI) of AIris.

Scavenger Hunt - IRL Word-Finding Game

We also implemented a scavenger hunt game to enhance learning. Kids can go outside, find objects, and take pictures using AIris. For example, during a Raya gathering, they can take a picture of “Baju Kurung,” and AIris will recognize the object and teach them about it. This feature is localized to Malaysian culture, helping kids learn about their heritage.

Scavenger Hunt User Interface (UI) of AIris.
Scavenger Hunt User Interface (UI) of AIris.

The Tech Stack We Used

  • Frontend:
    • Ionic Framework
  • Backend:
    • FastAPI
    • Google TTS and STT
  • Database:
    • PostgreSQL
    • CloudSQL
  • AI Model:
    • Gemini Model
  • Vector Database:
    • MongoDB
  • Deployment:
    • Google Cloud Platform
    • Firebase
    • Cloud Run
  • Version Control:
    • Git

Meet the Team

Here are five of my talented friends who made AIris possible. Feel free to connect with them and ask about their experiences with Gamuda AI Academy and full-stack development:

Name Role Professional Profile
Tan Ying Xin (Chris) Project Leader LinkedIn
Raina Radzaif Financial Strategy Leader LinkedIn
Daniel Koshy Product Designer LinkedIn
Muhammad Ihsan Salahuddin Software Tester LinkedIn
Lau Kee Wei AI Engineer LinkedIn

Conclusion

AIris is a fun and engaging application that helps kids learn Bahasa Melayu interactively. The use of cameras, flashcards, quizzes, leaderboards, and chatbots makes the learning experience enjoyable and effective. We are proud of what we achieved in such a short time and hope AIris positively impacts how kids learn languages in the future.

In the next post, I will share my personal experience during the demo day. Stay tuned!
entry.summary: A complete rundown of our capstone project, AIris.

entry.title: I joined Gamuda AI Academy!
entry.content:
My Personal Experience with Gamuda AI Academy

The first quarter of 2026 started on an unusual note for me. I enrolled in a fully funded program called Gamuda AI Academy. It felt surreal because, in today’s economy, nothing comes free anymore 🤣.

You can see how I am rocking with this red varsity jacket, and you guessed it, it is 100% free!

You also receive other free goodies, such as:

  • A tumbler
  • A Gamuda pin
  • Cute stickers
  • A laptop bag
  • A black Gamuda AI t-shirt

These thoughtful gifts made me feel welcomed from day one.

What is Gamuda AI Academy All About?

Gamuda AI Academy is a 3-month intensive bootcamp designed to prepare participants for a career in full-stack AI development. During the program, I learned essential tools and technologies, including:

  1. Frontend development with React
  2. Backend development with FastAPI
  3. Database management using PostgreSQL
  4. Cloud deployment on Google Cloud Platform
  5. AI integration with the Gemini API

The program dives deep into integrating AI functionality into software development. It also provides insights into how AI works under the hood and its potential to solve real-world problems. The trainers were incredibly supportive, always ready to answer questions and guide us through the learning process.

But that’s not all 🤯. The academy also focuses on enhancing soft skills! Weekly mentoring sessions covered topics like preparing for job interviews, excelling in public speaking, writing a standout resume, and even building your own startup company.

Here are some snapshots from the mentoring sessions during my cohort:

Friendly mentors sharing their knowledge and experiences during the sessions.

What Can You Gain from This Academy?

As an IT bootcamp, the biggest takeaway is the AI-powered capstone project you’ll complete. This project is perfect for showcasing your skills on LinkedIn, GitHub, or your resume. It serves as a great conversation starter during job interviews and demonstrates your ability to build AI-powered applications.

Additionally, the academy offers networking opportunities with industry experts from Gamuda. These sessions are invaluable for fresh graduates, providing insights into Gamuda’s culture, tips for improvement, and what to expect when applying to the company.

Here are some highlights from our site visits:

Visits to Gamuda Technologies, Google Malaysia, and Menara Gamuda!
A group photo of me and my fellow cute members!

My Thoughts on This Academy

Overall, I believe this academy is an excellent opportunity for anyone looking to dive into AI development or transition into the tech industry. While the program is intense, it offers invaluable experience in developing tech products with AI integration and solving real-world problems. The trainers and mentors are incredibly supportive, the learning environment is top-notch, and the community is highly encouraging. For me, this bootcamp deserves a ⭐ 10/10!

How to Apply

Applying is simple! Visit this link: Gamuda AI Academy and click on the “Apply Now” button. Be sure to read all the requirements carefully and understand what you’re signing up for.

Closing

Thank you for reading my first blog! I’ll be sharing more about my experiences at this academy soon. Stay tuned 🥳
entry.summary: A wonderful place to learn how to integrate AI into software development and solve real-world problems.
